answer.text: A bank authorized under FEMA to undertake all current and capital account transactions as directed by RBI
Any commercial bank registered with SEBI
A bank with assets over Rs. 10,000 crore
A bank that is a member of a stock exchange

explanation.text: **The Logic:** AD Category-I banks are scheduled commercial, state, or urban cooperative banks authorized under Section 10(1) of FEMA to undertake all current and capital account transactions per RBI directions. **The Trap:** AD license is under FEMA (not SEBI) and specifically for forex transactions. **Cross-Exam Context:** AD categories are tested in Series I. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** AD Cat-I banks can act as all three types of participants — hedgers (own exposure), speculators (proprietary trading), and arbitrageurs.
